output:
CVE-2020-35687 describes a Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) vulnerability in PHPFusion version 9.03.90, which could allow an attacker to delete all shoutbox messages on behalf of a logged-in user. This vulnerability stems from insufficient verification of whether a well-formed, valid request was intentionally provided by the user, as categorized under CWE-352.

The severity of this vulnerability is rated as Medium due to its potential impact on user data integrity, specifically targeting shoutbox messages. The vulnerability primarily affects PHPFusion version 9.03.90, allowing threat actors to perform unauthorized actions that users have not explicitly permitted.

Mitigation strategies for this type of vulnerability include implementing anti-CSRF tokens, ensuring strict verification processes for requests, and enhancing user session handling. According to the MITRE ATT&CK framework, this vulnerability aligns with the Exploit Public-Facing Application technique, ID T1190. Implementing recommended mitigation techniques such as M1048 and M1037 can improve security defenses against such attacks.
